body: Solid PR. The CoreSMT pipeline is well-structured with clean separation of concerns across State, IsCoreSMT, ExprTranslator, Diagnosis, StmtVerifier, and Verifier. The B3→Core→CoreSMT roundtrip with source location preservation is a meaningful improvement. A few issues to address before merging.

comments.body: Three imports are duplicated (lines 22-24 are repeated on lines 26-28). Remove the duplicate block.

Missing indentation on `| const` — inconsistent with the other cases:
```suggestion
  | const :  Value (.const _ _)
```

`DiagnosisInfo` is correctly placed next to `Outcome` and `VCResult`, but it references `Strata.Core.CoreSMT.DiagnosedFailure`, which forces `Core.Verifier` to import `CoreSMT.Diagnosis`. Consider extracting `DiagnosedFailure` and its supporting types (`DiagnosisResultType`, `DiagnosisContext`, `DiagnosisReport`, `DiagnosisResult`) into a new `Core.DiagnosisTypes` file that both `Core.Verifier` and `CoreSMT.Diagnosis` import, removing the dependency of the general verifier on the CoreSMT subsystem.

`TermType.toSMTString` is never called anywhere in the codebase. `SMTDDM.termTypeToString` and `Solver.typeToSMTString` already cover this conversion. Either use it (replacing one of the existing converters) or remove it.

`mkCvc5Solver`, `mkSolverFromPath`, and `mkSolverFromEnv` are defined but never called outside this file. If they are intended as a public API for future use, that's fine, but they could also be removed to keep the surface area minimal.

The second element of the pair in `results : List (VerificationReport × Option Unit)` is always `none` (set in `programToSMT`) and always ignored by callers. Consider simplifying to `List VerificationReport`.

This file is identical to `expected_incremental/test_incremental_simple.expected` and `run_py_analyze.sh` uses `expected_interactive/` for the incremental mode. The `expected_incremental/` directory is the one that appears orphaned — consider removing it, or renaming `expected_interactive/` to `expected_incremental/` for consistency with the `--incremental` flag name.
